5.6.2.  Theoretical Implications  
Current research has significant theoretical implications as it has contributed significantly to 
the literature of Resource based view. Resource based view is no more limited to have 
organisations’ focus on its internal resources only, rather it also emphasises on considering 
external sources of information as a significant resource for organisations that can contribute 
in enhancing organisational performance (Huo et al., 2016). Hence, in light of this view current 
research contributes further in the resource based view in a way that the findings of current 
research indicate that although manufacturing practices like TQM and TPM are implemented 
by organisations to manage and maintain the internal resources of the organisation to have 
positive outcomes from the operational performance of the organisation. But suppliers could 
also contribute as a significant resource so along with internal resource management; 
organisations should also focus on their external resources. Among all other external resources, 
suppliers are one of the most significant resources of organisation and they could enhance their 
operational performance through integration of this resource i.e. supplier integration.  
Moreover, as the resource based view emphasises that the resources of the organisation are the 
key determinants of organisational performance, so the findings of current research contribute 
in this view that suppliers as a significant resource of organisation also contribute towards 
operational performance of organisations and could also create a competitive advantage for the 
 
160 
 
organisation if firms go for integrated supply chains especially for supplier integration and the 
internal integration. More specifically the findings of current research contribute theoretically 
to the literature of resource-based view in following ways.  
5.6.2.1. 
The Impact of TPM/TQM on Operational Performance 
The resource-based view is based on the concept that organisational performance is determined 
by the resources of the organisation especially the way organisation utilises its resources for 
having a competitive edge and enhancing organisational performance. More specifically the 
resource-based view emphasises on the internal strengths and weaknesses of the organisation 
that could contribute in the development of the core competencies for organisation. But these 
competencies could be achieved only when these resources are not only identified theoretically 
but organisation should be able to utilise and gain benefit of these resources through practical 
implementation. 
The 
motivation 
behind 
this 
study 
is 
the 
realisation 
that 
Jordanian manufacturing organisations must raise the quality of their output if they are to 
increase productivity and become more competitive on the world market. Although several 
research have been conducted to examine the relationship between TQM and operational 
performance globally, very little, if anything.  
As the RBV focuses on the core competencies of the organisations by effective and efficient 
utilisation of its resources, hence the adoption and implementation of manufacturing practices 
like TQM and TPM also help organisations attaining such competencies. The findings of 
current research indicate that TQM is found to have significant positive effect on the flexibility 
performance while TPM is found to have significant positive effect on cost and flexibility 
performance of the organisation. It indicates that the manufacturing practices can help 
organisations in gaining the core competencies through effective and efficient utilisation of 
organisational resources. RBV has the major focus on the efficiency of organisations that how 
efficiently they utilise their resources, so the findings contribute that manufacturing practices 
help organisations in effective and efficient utilisation of these resources.   
TQM mainly focuses on elimination of waste as well as elimination of errors in the 
manufacturing and business operations of the organisations. So, the elimination of waste and 
errors lead towards effective and efficient utilisation of resources and enables the organisation 
to get better outputs from same inputs. TQM not only focuses on the manufacturing operations 
of the organisations, but it also emphasises on the stakeholders of the organisations like 
 
161 
 
efficient management of the supply chain at both ends as well as training and development of 
the employees of the organisation, so the findings of the study that TQM significantly 
positively contributes in operational performance of the organisation contribute that under the 
resource based view, organisations could better utilise their resources through implementation 
of the TQM practices in the organisation. TQM offers a procedure for managing dissatisfied 
customers, making necessary repairs, and preventing similar reoccurrences in addition to the 
quality assurance that consumers will receive what they want. the value of happy customers 
and the high cost of acquiring new consumers as compared to retaining existing ones. Customer 
satisfaction and revenue are directly correlated, according to business study (Nayal et al., 
2021). 
These findings of current research are consistent with the findings of previous studies; Yunis 
et al. (2013) indicated that TQM plays significant role in enhancing the operational 
performance of organisations. The author argued that in recent dynamic business environment, 
the TQM practices have become the vital need of organisations. Organisations now not only 
need to compete in their local markets, but globalisation has expanded the competition in 
international markets as well. Therefore, operational performance plays significant role in 
attaining organisational effectiveness and efficiency and enables the firm in international 
markets as well, while TQM practices help organisations in enhancing their operational 
performance. Zeng et al., (2015) indicated that TQM plays significant role in enhancing the 
operational performance of organisations in different perspectives but the most important one 
is its focus in enhancing the value of outcomes and gaining the competitive advantage. Nilda 
et al., (2017) indicated that organisations make significant efforts to have their operational 
performance according to the markets’ demands and wants, and TQM helps organisations in 
directing all their efforts for successful achievement of such goals.  
Current research also highlights the role of TPM which emphasises on the involvement of 
employees in the effective and efficient maintenance of the tools and machineries being utilised 
by the employees, so the tools and machines being the significant resources of organisation 
could be better utilised by the implementation of TPM in the organisation. As the findings of 
current research indicate that TPM significantly positively affects the operational performance 
of the organisation, so these findings contribute in RBV literature in a way that the tangible 
resources of the organisations i.e. the tools and machines could be best utilised and could 
 
162 
 
contribute in the enhancement of operational performance of organisation through 
implementation of the TPM practices in the organisations. 
These findings of current research are also consistent with the findings of previous studies; 
Chandran (2015) indicated that TPM improves the effectiveness and efficiency of production 
and manufacturing processes of the organisations. Madanhire and Mbohwa (2015) indicated 
that TPM optimises the operational efficiency of organisations through incorporation of 
employees in maintenance practices. Singh et al. (2013) indicated that TPM helps organisations 
enhancing their operational efficiency through ensuring the appropriate maintenance of tools 
and machines involved in the production operations and secondly through the implementation 
of correct procedures and corrective measures for enhancing the operational performance of 
organisation.  Similarly, Al Maazmiand and Al Hamadi (2021) reported that TPM helps 
organisations in enhancing their operational performance not only in terms of increased number 
of units but also in the form of improved quality of the products. In fact, TPM ensures the 
effective maintenance of equipment by involvement of concerned employees hence ensuring 
the elimination of breakdowns, accidents and defects. When such disruptions in the production 
process are eliminated the operational efficiency of the production units is ensured hence 
enhancing the operational performance of the organisation (Gupta et al., 2021).   
In this way the findings of current research contribute theoretically in a way that initially the 
resource-based view only emphasizes on the fact that the resources of organisation should be 
utilised in most efficient manner, but the findings of current research also indicate the ways 
that how these resources could be best utilised and how the outcomes of these resources could 
be enhanced i.e., through adoption and implementation of the manufacturing practice (TPM, 
TQM).   
5.6.2.2. 
The Impact of Supplier/Internal Integration on TPM/TQM 
As the findings of current research indicate the significant relationships between the 
independent variables of study i.e. TPM, TQM, supplier and internal integration, so these 
findings also contribute theoretically in the literature of RBV. The findings indicate that 
supplier integration has significant positive effect on TPM, and TQM and similar positive 
effect of internal integration is found on TPM and TQM as well. As mentioned earlier that the 
major focus of RBV is the effective utilisation of the resources of organisation and initially the 
RBV was based on focuses on the internal resources of the organisations, but with the passage 
 
163 
 
of time the view has also extended that resources are not limited to the internal factors of the 
organisations, but the flow of information should also be among the internal and external 
stakeholders of the organisations (Huo et al., 2016).  
Therefore, in light of this view suppliers are also considered as a significant resource of 
organisations that not only contribute to providing effective information about the market and 
materials but also facilitate the organisations in efficient management of inventory that leads 
to have financial and non-financial benefits for the organisations. (Gupta et al., 2021). So, the 
findings of current research contribute that the effective and efficient utilisation of resources 
could be achieved through the implementation of manufacturing practices that help 
organisation in enhancing the outputs through elimination of errors and improving the efficient 
utilisation of tools and machines by the employees, while this effectiveness and efficiency is 
further enhanced through internal and supplier integration.  
Although TPM and TQM enhance the efficiency of business operations, but this efficiency 
could never be achieved if these practices are implemented at individual departmental levels, 
rather internal integration i.e., the collaborative efforts of the internal departments of the 
organisations would lead to get better results in the form of elimination of errors and better 
performance of tools and machines through efficient maintenance. The integration of 
departments facilitates the flow of information among the concerned individuals, and they 
could better resolve issues through effective discussion and feedback from the concerned 
authorities and individuals (Gupta et al., 2021).  
Hence, as the resource-based view emphasises on the effective and efficient utilisation of 
organisational resources so internal integration helps achieving this efficiency by enhancing 
the outcomes of TPM and TQM practices. Moreover, effective and efficient utilisation also 
implies to get better outcomes from available inputs, so supplier integration helps organisations 
having significant control on their inputs i.e., inventory management etc. As the TQM focuses 
on eliminating errors and wastes of the organisation so that enhanced outputs could be gained 
through similar inputs and TPM focuses on the utilisation of tools and machines and their 
maintenance to improve their productive efficiency, so supplier integration contributes 
positively in a way that it facilitates organisations to have significant control on the inventory 
levels hence helps eliminating unnecessary levels of inputs (Gupta et al., 2021). Similarly, 
when inventory levels are just according to the requirements of the organisation and the 
 
164 
 
demand of the markets, then excessive production is also controlled, and tools and machines 
are not over run hence TPM is efficiently implemented as well. 
Therefore, in this way supplier and internal integration contribute positively towards TPM and 
TQM and also contribute in RBV literature because these integrations help organisations in 
effective and efficient utilisation of organisational resources. Again, as discussed earlier that 
RBV emphasises on effective utilisation of resources, so the findings of current research 
contribute by providing ways of such effective utilisation. Moreover, the findings of current 
research also contribute theoretically to the literature of manufacturing practices and SCM as 
these two approaches are always compared by the researchers in different perspectives, but 
their joint effects are always ignored by the previous studies. Marian et al., (2020) while making 
comparison among the manufacturing practices (TPM, TQM) and the SCM reported that 
manufacturing practices (TPM, TQM) aim at enhancing the quality of outputs through 
enhancement of operational performance while SCM helps controlling the manufacturing 
processes with a focus of quality enhancement. Thus, both approaches have shared purpose of 
quality enhancement approached in different ways. Similarly, Talib, et al. (2011) indicated that 
SCM and TQM have become vital for organisations’ success hence organisations adopting any 
of these approaches could achieve their goals in better way than the ones who are not 
implementing such approaches. These studies lack reporting the combined effect of these 
approaches as well as have not focused on the facts that if organisations are adopting both 
approaches in a single setting then how they are able to enhance their operational efficiency. 
So, the findings of current research contribute to filling this gap.  
On the other hand, some previous studies (Nilda et al. 2017; Yunis, 2013; Rachmat, 2019) have 
reported the interrelationship of supplier and internal integration with the manufacturing 
practices, so the findings of current research validate the findings of these previous studies. 
Nilda et al. (2017) reported that the effectiveness of TQM practices is enhanced by internal 
integration in a way that when different functions within the organisation work collaboratively 
then they all line up for achievement of a single goal of quality improvement. Yunis (2013) 
reported that manufacturing practices are adopted by the organisations to cope with the 
challenges of market dynamics; hence supplier integration improves the effectiveness of these 
manufacturing practices by providing appropriate information, control of costs and 
consolidating the processes in efficient manners. Rachmat (2019) indicated that 
implementation of manufacturing practices requires training of employees for ensuring the 
 
165 
 
effectiveness of these practices, so internal integration improves the effectiveness of such 
training by facilitating the flow of information among different functional units of the 
organisation. Hence, all such studies have reported the effects of supplier and internal 
integration on manufacturing practices (TPM, TQM), which are also validated by the findings 
of current research, but current research further contributes in literature that how these two 
approaches jointly contribute towards improvement of the operational performance of the 
organisation.   
5.6.2.3. 
The Impact of Supplier/Internal Integration on Operational Performance 
Findings from this research indicate that supplier integration is found to have significant 
positive effect on cost and flexibility performance of the organisation, while internal integration 
is found to have significant positive effect on flexibility performance of the organisation. 
Operational performance is considered as an indicator of the success of organisation as it helps 
achieving the desired and targeted goals, so any factors contributing positively to enhancing 
this operational performance are also considered as significant resources of the organisation. 
The major focus of RBV for effective utilisation of organisational resources is further enhanced 
through the findings of current research in a way that organisational departments and suppliers 
are the significant resources of organisations, but these could be best utilised through 
integration. As supplier integration is found to have positive effect on cost performance so it is 
indicated that through supplier integration organisations could have better control on the costs 
of their operations.  
Cost efficiency could be a competitive advantage for the organisations so through supplier 
integration organisations could gain the core competencies as emphasised by the RBV. 
Moreover, supplier integration is also found to have significant positive effect on the flexibility 
performance of the organisation, and in recent dynamic environment operational flexibility has 
become essential for the survival of organisations due to frequent changes in the marketplaces. 
Organisations having operational flexibility could gain the core competencies in making 
modifications and adjustments in their operations according to the changes in market demands 
and supplier integration plays significant role in attaining this core competency. Having healthy 
relationships with suppliers and incorporating them in the decision-making stages could help 
organisations in achieving the operational flexibility.  
 
166 
 
In this way, the findings of current research also validate the findings of previous research 
(Wieland et al., 2016; Gurahoo and Salisbury, 2018; Ahmad et al., 2019). Wieland et al. (2016) 
reported that internal functional units of the organisations are critical resources for attaining 
the targeted outcomes of the organisations, but their collaborative efforts are necessary for 
eliminating the inefficiencies of operations, so internal integration helps organisations in 
ensuring the collaborative efforts of these functional units which ultimately helps improving 
the operational performance of organisations. Ahmad et al. (2019) indicated that when internal 
departments of the organisations are integrated then they all work together for the achievement 
of desired goals. So, employees’ integration creates competitive advantage for the organisation 
and helps achieving the goals related to cost, quality, and responsiveness. Gurahoo and 
Salisbury (2018) indicated that supplier integration incorporates different stakeholders working 
together for achievement of desired value of the outputs. Such integration helps organisations 
in accurate assessment of the risks and opportunities prevailing in the external environment 
and helps mitigating the uncertainty of the business environment and contributes positively in 
enhancement of the operational performance.  
Hence, the findings of current research not only contribute in expanding the approaches of 
resource based view but also contribute significantly to the literature of SCM, TQM and TPM. 
It also provides insights to the researchers for investigating the relationships among factors in 
different perspectives.  
5.6.3. Practical Implications 
Current research has not only significant theoretical implications but also have practical 
implications for industrial organisations especially for Jordanian industrial organisations. As 
highlighted by the review of previous literature that initially the manufacturing practices (TPM, 
TQM) and the supply chain integration are found to have significant contribution towards 
operational performance of the organisations, but these were focused individually like some 
organisations were focusing on implementation of manufacturing practices for enhancement of 
their operational performance or the overall performance of the organisation. While some 
others were focusing on the supply chain integration for enhancing the operational efficiency 
of the organisations, but neither researchers nor industrialists have focused on adoption of both 
strategies in single settings due to the lack of investigation in this area.  
In this regard, current research has provided significant insights for the industrialists by 
highlighting the interrelationships of these practices and strategies. Industrial organisations’ 
 
167 
 
efficiency and success depends on their operational efficiency and in the context of Jordan cost 
and flexibility are found to be the main determinants of the operational performance of 
industrial organisations. Hence, current research provides insight to Jordanian industrialists 
that which factors contribute positively to enhancing cost and flexibility performance of these 
organisations.  
The positive impact of TPM and TQM on operational performance of the organisation as found 
by the results of current research indicate that Jordanian industrialist could enhance their 
operational performance by adoption and implementation of manufacturing practices (TPM 
and TQM). Operational performance efficiency could be a competitive edge for these 
industrialists when they would have better controls on their internal resources; they are able to 
eliminate waste and errors in the operations so that they could be able to get higher levels of 
outputs from similar levels of inputs. Moreover, through the implementation of TPM practices 
they could enable their employees to effective and efficiently utilising their tools and machines 
in a way that these could provide enhanced outputs. So, the implementation of manufacturing 
practices could help them gaining the efficiency in operational performance. 
On the other side, the supplier and internal integration is found to have positive impact on the 
manufacturing practices, so Jordanian industrialists should also focus on developing supply 
chain integration especially supplier integration and internal integration. Currently in Jordan, 
supplier and internal integration is not widely implemented in the industrial firms, so the 
findings of current research provide insight to Jordanian industrialists that they should not only 
focus on implementation of the manufacturing practices, but they should also focus on supplier 
and internal integration. Internal integration would help their employees having a better 
understanding of the operations and to resolve issues in an efficient manner that would 
ultimately contribute positively to enhancing operational performance of the organisation. 
Moreover, if supplier integration is developed then they would be able to have better 
performance in terms of cost efficiency and operational flexibility. Just like all other economies 
across globe, Jordanian industries are also moving in the dynamic environment so they also 
need to have operational flexibility so that they can cope with the dynamic business 
environment. Hence, supplier integration helps attaining this operational efficiency so 
Jordanian industrialists should focus on developing internal and supplier integration so that 
they could enhance their operational performance as well.  
 
168 
 
The current research is expected to help Jordanian industrialists to have a practical implication 
of the resource-based view in a way that the RBV has the main focus on effective utilisation of 
organisational resources, so current research provides insights to the industrialists that how 
they could utilise their internal and external resources in best possible way. In recent dynamic 
environment, the flow of information at internal levels of organisations as well as from external 
to internal levels of organisations has become vital for the survival of organisations, and this 
flow of information could be developed through integration at different levels of the supply 
chain. As Jordanian economy and Jordanian firms have somehow rigid environments which 
lack flexibility and integration, so current research emphasises that Jordanian industrialists 
should focus on integration of supply chain especially internal and supplier integration 
otherwise they would not be able to enhance their operational performance.  
The success of industrial organisations is dependent on the operational efficiency of the 
organisations, so organisations could not survive while ignoring the important factors that 
contribute significantly in enhancement of this operational performance. Similarly, in the 
context of Jordan, as current research has found that TPM, TQM, supplier integration and 
internal integration are significantly contributing towards operational performance of the 
organisations, so Jordanian industries could not survive while focusing on manufacturing 
practices or SC integration separately. Rather they should now focus on adoption of both these 
strategies at the same time so that they could be able to gain the competitive advantage.  
5.7.  Chapter Summary 
The current research utilized a path analysis in order to test the hypotheses of the model, where 
the study model was designed, which consists of four independent variables (TPM, TQM, 
Supplier, and Internal) and two dependent variables (Cost, Flexibility) in the Jordanian 
industrial companies, where all the results were presented in this chapter, which were 
acceptable. The results of the analysis will be discussed in detail in the next chapter. 
Additionally, the chapter explains the results of the descriptive analysis of the data collected 
from the study sample, which is represented by the Jordanian industrial firms. The chapter 
explained the logic behind conducting the different tests to check the normality, validity, and 
reliability of the study data. After running the different quantitative tests using SPSS and 
AMOS software, all obtained values from the data analysis process were within the acceptable 
ratios. In that, they obtained values have passed the stability coefficient test of Cronbach’s 
 
169 
 
alpha as well as the exploratory factor analysis and the confirmatory factor analysis tests. Based 
on this, it can be confirmed that the research model is fit, and the obtained values are valid. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
170 
 
Chapter 6 – Conclusion 
After exploring the literature and discussing with academic experts, the quantitative method 
was favoured to investigate the research phenomenon and to provide generalisable findings 
and conclusions. For that, both traditional and online forms of questionnaire were distributed 
to collect data from firms operating in the Jordanian industrial sector. Many firms have 
responded to the questionnaire, and after the filtration process, 224 responses were adopted. 
The data from these questionnaires were descriptively analysed and tested (normality, EFA, 
CFA, regression) using SPSS and AMOS software. Based on these analysis steps, the different 
research hypotheses were tested, which allowed producing twenty-one findings that were 
presented in six models. These results were then discussed and thus, collectively answered the 
main research question: 
RQ: What impact do manufacturing practices (TPM, TQM) have on the operational 
performance of the industrial firms when adopted in line with supplier and internal 
integration? 
This thesis showed that manufacturing practices are very important in the business world, thus, 
they deserve to be focused on by researchers. The thesis discussed the impact of manufacturing 
practices (TPM, TQM) on operational performance (cost, flexibility). It also clarified the 
importance of SCM elements (supplier integration, internal integration) in enhancing this 
impact in industrial firms. This research has significant contribution in literature as well as in 
the economic development of Jordanian economy. The literature lacked evidence about 
reporting the impact of the combined effect of manufacturing practices and the SCM practices 
to enhance the operational performance of the organizations. This study has filled this gap. It 
highlighted the effect of TPM, TQM along with supplier integration and internal integration on 
operational performance of industries. By studying these effects in Jordanian economy, the 
study has contributed to Jordanian literature and knowledge of the policy makers that how these 
factors can enhance the operational performance of Jordanian industrial organisations. 
Although industrial organisations in Jordan are already practices such approaches to enhance 
their operational performance but the combined effects of these approaches are never 
implemented and focused by these organisations. Therefore, this study has significant practical 
implications as well. Following the findings of this research, Jordanian industrialists can focus 
 
171 
 
on integration and accumulated implementations of the approaches so that enhanced outcomes 
can be generated through operational efficiencies.  
The remainder of this chapter contains a quick overview of the various research findings, its 
strengths, limitations, and recommendations for future research efforts. 
6.1. Overview of Research 
In order to find out the impact of manufacturing practices (TPM, TQM) on operational 
performance (Cost, Flexibility) of the firms while investigating the role of supplier and internal 
integration in this relationship, current research has incorporated Jordanian industrial firms 
through collection of primary data from the concerned participants. Although the relationship 
of manufacturing practices and SC integration has already been studied by previous research 
in the same field, but these studies lacked investigating the interrelationships of manufacturing 
practices and SC integration towards operational performance of the organisations. Especially 
in the context of Jordanian firms, two manufacturing practices i.e., TPM and TQM were 
identified as significant contributors towards operational performance, and in SC integration, 
supplier integration and internal integration were found to have more contribution as compared 
to the customer integration, so current research has focused on these specific factors only. 
Moreover, for operational performance, the cost and flexibility performance are found to be 
the key determinants of operational performance of Jordanian industries.  
The investigation of the relationships among all these variables indicated that manufacturing 
practices (TPM, TQM) are positively contributing towards operational performance. Although 
TQM is not found to have significant relationship with the cost performance, but other variables 
are found to have significant positive contribution. On the other hand, supplier and internal 
integration are also found to have positive contribution towards operational performance of the 
organisations. Among these variables, internal integration is not found to have significant 
relationship with the cost performance of the organisation, but it is found to have significant 
relationship with the flexibility performance of the organisations.  
While studying the interrelationships of supplier and internal integration with the 
manufacturing practice, it is found that supplier and internal integration have positive role 
towards TPM and TQM. So, the findings of current research indicate that Jordanian industries 
should not only focus on implementation of the manufacturing practices for enhancing their 
operational performance, but they should also focus on developing internal and supplier 
 
172 
 
integration. As the current research has investigated these relationships in light of the RBV, so 
it contributes significantly to literature by highlighting that organisations should not only focus 
on management and maintenance of the internal resources of the organisation, but these should 
also consider external factors i.e., suppliers as significant resource and effective utilisation of 
these resources could enhance the operational performance of these organisations.  
6.2. Research Strengths 
Every research work contains several strengths and limitations. While limitations will be the 
focus of later discussion, this section highlights the research strengths and how they provide 
extra value for its outcomes. This research has covered the Jordanian industrial sector and 
obtained responses from a large group of firms that are usually difficult to access. The research 
has gained this access after working on developing several contacts with different Jordanian 
authorities and institutions in order to guarantee their support. These include the Ministry of 
Industry, Trade and Supply and Amman Chamber of Industry. This resulted in the ability of 
this research to access a poorly explored and nearly unlocked sector to dig deeper and 
understand the various factors that affect its operational performance. 
This research has covered an area that is usually less interesting for researchers globally. To 
explain, while research efforts in this field usually focus on exploring the manufacturing 
practises and their role in improving the operational performance of organisations in the well-
developed, Western economies, this thesis has focused on investigating the Jordanian industrial 
sector that is considered to be a developing, middle eastern economy. The research, therefore, 
has produced findings that can provide a steppingstone for other research efforts in the same 
area or other areas. 
Limited studies have focused on manufacturing practises and the operational performance with 
supply chain integration as a moderating factor. However, the Jordanian industrial sector has 
been suffering in the last years from the difficulty of securing the industrial logistics, especially 
with several regional circumstances affecting the ability to import the raw material and export 
final goods. As a developing country, it was necessary to highlight the importance of properly 
implementing the manufacturing practices such as TQM and TPM in order to enhance the 
quality, and reduce the time, of production. With this in mind, this research provides a solution 
that can help stakeholders in the industrial sector to make thoughtful decisions to produce more 
unique products with a competitive quality, which enables the Jordanian industrial firms to 
maximise their profitability. 
 
173 
 
6.3. Research Limitations 
This research has encountered some limitations during the process of data collection. As this 
research was conducted based on the quantitative survey tool, and as the world today is going 
through the covid-19 pandemic, more access to data with few more participating firms would 
increase the reliability and credibility of the findings. This limitation was addressed by 
distributing online questionnaires to participants in order to secure more responses.  
In many cases, some terms and concepts were ambiguous to participants and, thus, required to 
be further clarified to guarantee the high accuracy of answers. Although some responses were 
excluded because of the poor answers that occurred from misunderstanding the included terms 
and concepts, other responses were completed by explaining the intended meanings of these 
terms and concepts to participants. As in any research discussing such technical topic, this issue 
is expected to occur, but, fortunately, it was overcoming by adopting the right communications 
with participants. Another limitation of this research is to not highlight the differences of effect 
in different kinds of settings i.e., SMEs and MNCs. As the SMEs and MNCs vary in their 
structures and functioning, so the effects of these manufacturing and supply chain practices 
will be different in these two different settings. These are not highlighted by this research. 
These could be highlighted in future research.  
6.4. Research Recommendations 
The results of this research show that there is an effect of manufacturing practices on flexibility. 
This, in turn, encourages the first future research avenue about the need to studying the other 
factors of manufacturing practices that were not addressed in this thesis in order to provide 
more comprehensive view of this variable. As there was no effect of TPM on cost and 
flexibility, future works should investigate the reasons for the absence of any relationship 
between these variables. While this research has focused on the scale of industrial firms, other 
research efforts should study the same variables in other sectors and areas to find out about the 
ability of replicating the current findings.  
Further, conducting more studies about the other factors of supply chains management such as 
customer integration rather than supplier integration and internal integration can be useful to 
check their role, if any; in enhancing the operational performance of industrial firms (the same 
idea applies to factors related to manufacturing practices). In addition, future research avenues 
might consider the impact of manufacturing practices on the financial performance, as opposed 
 
174 
 
to the operational performance, of the industrial firms. Furthermore, the research can be 
conducted on finding out the impact of these combined practices in different types of 
organisational settings. The research could also be conducted in finding out the differences in 
different kinds of industries.  
6.5. Chapter Summary 
This research has developed a framework about the impact of manufacturing practices (TPM, 
TQM) on the operational performance (cost, flexibility) of the industrial firms. The research 
further has focused on the factors of SCM (supplier integration, internal integration) as 
moderators of this previously explained relationship. The research, thus, has fulfilled its aim 
of identifying and better understanding the role of having proper manufacturing practices in 
enhancing the operational performance of industrial organisations, and how this role is 
promoted with the presence of the right supply chain integration.
